title: Set a "status" column to the current Month when item is created
excerpt: I want a status column with Months. Whenever a new item is created, the current month is set for that item.  I can’t figure out a way to make this happen.  It seems simple, but nothing I’ve tried has worked.

posts.author: traveler (Dean Fowler)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want a status column with Months. Whenever a new item is created, the current month is set for that item. I can’t figure out a way to make this happen. It seems simple, but nothing I’ve tried has worked.

image534×575 24.6 KB

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I am not aware of a native solution to this. I believe third party apps would be able to fulfill this though. Is that a solution you’d be open to?

posts.author: traveler (Dean Fowler)
posts.cookedHtml:
No… it’s not really that important. It just seemed like there would be an easy solution I was missing.

I did see there was a 3rd party app I could use to cast a formula to a number field. That would’ve allowed me to create additional automations to get what I needed, but we’re not interested in adding 3rd party apps.

Regards

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: For those who would be interested in a 3rd party solution, the Advanced Formula Booster could do it easily.

posts.author: cajaygle (Jay Cagle)
posts.cookedHtml: I do something like this on my board. Make a Start Date column that uses an automation to set the value to the current date when the item is created. Then have a formula column that calculates the Month by using MONTH({Start Date})

posts.author: traveler (Dean Fowler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jay,

I did something similar and can calculate the value of the month with the formula. But formula columns are not an option for automations to change a status column. It requires a number column. If I could convert/cast the formula to a number column, it would work in theory.

Thanks!

posts.author: cajaygle (Jay Cagle)
posts.cookedHtml: Why does the Month need to be a status column? What are you trying to do with the month column after it’s been set?

posts.author: traveler (Dean Fowler)
posts.cookedHtml: For a Kaban view of the information as well as a dashboard view.

posts.author: cajaygle (Jay Cagle)
posts.cookedHtml: I see. yeah, I don’t see a way to do that natively. It would be nice if an automation could set a timeline column. good luck!
